title: senna italica subsp. arachoides
intro: Senna italica subsp. arachoides, also known as Arachis senna or Italian senna, is a plant species within the legume family Fabaceae, native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its laxative and anti-inflammatory effects, due to its high sennoside content. The health benefits of Senna italica subsp. arachoides include its potential to treat constipation, diarrhea, and various gastrointestinal issues, as well as its use in traditional medicine to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Herbal preparations of Senna italica subsp. arachoides can be found in the form of dried leaves or roots, and are often used in teas, infusions, or as a powder, although its use should be cautious and under medical supervision due to potential side effects.
